paper_title: % % PhageBench: Evaluating LLM Reasoning Directly on Raw Bacteriophage Genomes % PhageBench: Can LLMs Understand and Reason Over Raw Bacteriophage Genomes? %

P000_S0000.reason: The surrounding context shows \yes should map to $\checkmark$ and \no to $\times$; swapping makes \yes mean ✗, contradicting its semantic name.

P001_S0001.reason: The surrounding context shows \no should map to $\times$; changing it to $\checkmark$ contradicts the semantic meaning of the command name.

P002_S0002.reason: Related passages consistently use $\dagger$ for co-first author superscripts and the footnote definition, contradicting the perturbed $\ddagger$.

P003_S0003.reason: Related passages confirm $\dagger$ is used for both co-first authors (Yusen Hou and Weicai Long), contradicting the perturbed $\ast$.

P004_S0004.reason: Related passages show the footnote uses $\dagger$ to mark co-first authors, contradicting the perturbed $\ast$ in the footnote definition.

P005_S0005.reason: Related passages use $\Delta$ consistently in table headers and the caption definition, contradicting the perturbed $\nabla$.

P006_S0006.reason: The caption states 'Positive values indicate CoT improvement,' which only holds for Acc_CoT - Acc_0-shot, not the reversed formula.

P007_S0007.reason: Related passages and the caption definition use plain $\Delta$ throughout the table headers, contradicting the added subscript 10.

P008_S0008.reason: Related passages use plain $\Delta$ in all table column headers, and the caption defines $\Delta$ positively as CoT improvement, contradicting $-\Delta$.

P009_S0009.reason: Related passages use plain $\Delta$ in all table column headers without any subscript, contradicting the added _avg subscript.
